name: California Speech-Language Pathologist License
description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to California - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology and Hearing Aid Dispensers Board

address.description:
Speech-Language Pathology and Audiology and Hearing Aid Dispensers Board
2005 Evergreen Street, Suite 2100
Sacramento, CA 95815-3817

requiredDocuments.name: Official graduate transcripts
requiredDocuments.description: From a CAA-accredited speech-language pathology program.

requiredDocuments.name: Praxis exam score report
requiredDocuments.description: Score of 162 or higher on test 5331. Must be dated within the last five years.

requiredDocuments.name: RPE completion verification
requiredDocuments.description: Signed by supervising licensed SLP confirming 36 weeks full-time or 72 weeks part-time.

requiredDocuments.name: Fingerprint background check
requiredDocuments.description: Live Scan fingerprinting submitted to the Department of Justice and FBI.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a California SLP license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: The total initial licensure cost is $150. This includes a $35 RPE temporary license application fee and $115 for the permanent license application. Biennial renewal is $150.

mainEntity.name: Does California accept the ASHA CCC-SLP for licensure?

acceptedAnswer.text: Holding the ASHA CCC-SLP provides an alternative pathway to California SLP licensure. CCC-SLP holders may qualify for expedited review, but must still submit a full California application with all required documentation and fees.

mainEntity.name: Can I practice telepractice as an SLP in California?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. California permits licensed SLPs to provide telepractice services. You must hold a valid California SLP license to treat clients located in California, and all telepractice services must meet the same standard of care as in-person treatment.

mainEntity.name: Does California have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?

acceptedAnswer.text: No interstate compact currently covers speech-language pathologist practice in California. To work in California, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to California?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 weeks.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in California while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
